chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Const. Petition No. S – 104 of 2026 __________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) Hearing of case 1. For orders on office objection Nos.1 to 4 a/w reply as at “A” 2. For hearing of main case 16.02.2026 Mr. Rahim Ali Rind, Advocate along with petitioner. Mr. Faheem Hussain, DPG. Mr. Sagheer Ahmed Abbasi, AAG along with Addl. SHO / SIP Mian Bux, PI Muhammad Nawaz, PC Yousif, PS Dhabeji, SIP Naseem Khan, PS Shah Latif Town and Raza Mian, DSP Legal-II, CPO. ------------------------------------ Addl. SHO / SIP Mian Bux is present on behalf of Mubeen Parhyar, SHO PS Dhabeji District Thatta on the ground that the latter is not feeling wail. Report/ comments submitted by SDPO Malir, SHO PS Dhabeji and SHO PS Shah Lateef Town are taken on record. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that Mobeen Parhyar, the respondent No.5 / SHO PS Dhabeji had extorted an amount of Rs.15,00,000/- from the petitioner and to such effect a petition bearing C.P. No. S-1298 of 2025 was filed, which petition by way of order dated 28.11.2025 was disposed of with directions to the respondents not to harass the petitioner. Learned counsel further adds that the anxiety of the petitioner was that the SHO PS Dhabeji had illegally obtained a substantial amount of Rs.15,00,000/- and no directions were issued in the above said petition, therefore, he approached the IGP Sindh and vide WT Message dated 12.12.2025 (page 17 of the Court file), Mubeen Parhyar, the SHO PS Dhabeji, SIP Hajan, DPC Yousif Arain, PC Shameer and PC Sheeraz, PS Dhabeji, District Thatta were directed to appear before the SSP Matiari/ Enquiry Officer.

S-1298 of 2025 was filed, which petition by way of order dated 28.11.2025 was disposed of with directions to the respondents not to harass the petitioner. Learned counsel further adds that the anxiety of the petitioner was that the SHO PS Dhabeji had illegally obtained a substantial amount of Rs.15,00,000/- and no directions were issued in the above said petition, therefore, he approached the IGP Sindh and vide WT Message dated 12.12.2025 (page 17 of the Court file), Mubeen Parhyar, the SHO PS Dhabeji, SIP Hajan, DPC Yousif Arain, PC Shameer and PC Sheeraz, PS Dhabeji, District Thatta were directed to appear before the SSP Matiari/ Enquiry Officer. Learned counsel further submits that the officers including respondent No.5 had not appeared before the Enquiry Officer on the pretext that said SHO has specific terms with the ruling party, hence all the SSPs even IGP Sindh have no authority to disturb the said SHO. Record reveals in spite of directions, the said SHO PS Dhabeji is not in attendance today before this Court. 2 Learned counsel further submits that the petitioner was threatened by the SHO PS Dhabeji that in the event the extortion amount has not been paid to him again then he will be implicating him in any false criminal case. In the circumstances, a report be called from the SSP Mitiari in respect of the officers / officials mentioned in WT Message whether they have appeared before him or not and in case they have appeared before him, what is the result of enquiry entrusted to him. The SSP Mitiari, subject to approval of the IGP Sindh, shall conduct the enquiry after providing equal opportunity of hearing to all corner including the petitioner. The SSP Thatta is directed to produce Mubeen Parhyar, the SHO PS Dhabeji before this Court on the next date of hearing. In case of failure, he shall remain present before this Court along with his explanation. A copy of this order shall be sent by fax to the IGP Sindh, SSP Thatta and SSP Matiari for compliance. In so far as the officers / officials present today are concerned, since they have submitted their respective reports / comments and submit that they will not harass the petitioner in future except due course of law, hence at the request of learned AAG their further appearance is dispensed with unless so directed.

A copy of this order shall be sent by fax to the IGP Sindh, SSP Thatta and SSP Matiari for compliance. In so far as the officers / officials present today are concerned, since they have submitted their respective reports / comments and submit that they will not harass the petitioner in future except due course of law, hence at the request of learned AAG their further appearance is dispensed with unless so directed. To come up on 02.03.2026. MIT-II to ensure compliance. JUDGE HEAD OF CONST. BENCHES Azeem
